pattern: Split each cell into four triangular quadrants and union them according to the slash, then merge across cell borders; the number of disjoint sets is the region count.
hints: A slash inside a unit cell cuts it into pieces, so think about subdividing each cell rather than the cell as a whole.
Give each cell four triangles (top, right, bottom, left); a blank joins all four, a slash or backslash joins them in two pairs.
Union the four triangles per cell by its character, then union each cell's right triangle with the left of its neighbor and bottom with the top below; count remaining components.

edge_inputs.description: a lone slash leaves one region
single blank cell is one region
single slash cell is one region
parallel slashes split into three regions

stress.description: random small grids over all three cell characters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10))
    chars = [' ', '/', '\\']
    grid = [''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(n)) for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: backslash escaped once for the JSON string literal
